title: GCS: Motor Response
subtitle: Give a command, apply trapezius squeeze when required and record the best motor response.

cards.title: Assess motor response
cards.frontPrompt: How should you begin the motor component of the GCS?
backPoints: Ask the patient to lift both arms.
Allow enough time for the patient to respond.
If the patient obeys the command, record M6.
Apply trapezius squeeze only if the patient does not obey.
Record the best motor response demonstrated.
cards.memoryLine: Command first. Pain only if required.

cards.title: Localising and flexion withdrawal
cards.frontPrompt: How do you distinguish M5 from M4?
cards.backHeading: Look at the direction of movement
backPoints: M5 localises: the patient reaches towards the stimulus and attempts to remove it.
M4 flexion withdrawal: the arm flexes and moves away from the painful stimulus.
Flexion withdrawal does not include reaching towards the examiner's hand.
cards.memoryLine: Towards the stimulus is M5. Away from the stimulus is M4.

cards.title: Abnormal flexion and extension
cards.frontPrompt: How do M3 and M2 differ?
backPoints: M3: abnormal flexion of the arms towards the chest.
M2: extension of the arms in response to painful stimulation.
M1: no motor response is observed.
cards.memoryLine: M3 flexes abnormally. M2 extends. M1 has no response.

assessment.instructions: Choose how to assess the patient. Give a command, apply trapezius squeeze when required, then select the correct GCS motor score.

actions.label: Ask patient to lift both arms

commandResponse.text: The patient lifts both arms on request.

painResponse.text: The patient has already obeyed the command. Painful stimulation was not required.

commandResponse.text: The patient does not lift either arm.

painResponse.text: During trapezius squeeze, the patient reaches towards your hand and attempts to remove the stimulus.

commandResponse.text: The patient does not lift either arm.

painResponse.text: During trapezius squeeze, the patient flexes the arm and withdraws away from the painful stimulus.

commandResponse.text: The patient does not lift either arm.

painResponse.text: During trapezius squeeze, the arms flex abnormally towards the chest.

commandResponse.text: The patient does not lift either arm.

painResponse.text: During trapezius squeeze, the arms extend in response to the stimulus.

commandResponse.text: The patient does not lift either arm.

painResponse.text: There is no motor response to trapezius squeeze.

feedback.correct: Correct. The patient's best motor response is M{score}.
feedback.incorrect: The response demonstrated corresponds to M{score}.
feedback.painNotRequired: The patient obeyed the command, so painful stimulation was not required.
feedback.commandRequired: Assess the patient's response to a command before applying painful stimulation.
feedback.assessmentIncomplete: Complete the motor assessment before selecting a score.
